title: invitations — third-party subprocessors
description: Third-party services this module uses. Aggregated at repo root by the compliance module's registry (GDPR Art. 28). Each entry names the purpose, region, DPA link, and data classes touched. Only one is required in production — the mail transport — but this module supports the whole list so workspaces can pick per region + preference.

subprocessors.name: SendGrid
subprocessors.purpose: Transactional email delivery for invitation messages. Primary provider on the `academorix.app` US application. Reports delivery + open + click + bounce + complaint events via inbound webhook.
data_classes: confidential (invitee email + name + inviter display name)

subprocessors.name: Amazon SES
subprocessors.purpose: Transactional email — primary provider on the `academorix.eu` EU application (data-residency requirement). SNS-based delivery / bounce / complaint webhooks.
subprocessors.signature_verification: AWS SNS signature verification via aws-sdk-php

subprocessors.name: Postmark
subprocessors.purpose: Transactional email — enterprise-tier workspace option for the highest deliverability rate (Postmark's US IP reputation is exceptional). Used where a specific workspace opts in via the `mail_transport` workspace setting.

subprocessors.name: Mailgun
subprocessors.purpose: Transactional email — third-tier fallback + on-demand workspace option (EU + US IP pools).

subprocessors.name: Twilio
subprocessors.purpose: SMS delivery for the `sms` channel. Optional per-workspace (channel gated by `invitations.channels.sms` entitlement).
subprocessors.region: global — routes to the recipient's region automatically

subprocessors.name: Slack
subprocessors.purpose: Slack delivery for the `slack` channel. Optional — workspaces configure a bot user + webhook URL per workspace they want to invite into.
subprocessors.region: us + eu (per workspace Slack workspace's data-residency setting)
data_classes: confidential (invitee email if provided, inviter display name)

operational_notes: Exactly ONE mail transport is active per Application at any time (from SendGrid / SES / Postmark / Mailgun). Twilio + Slack are additive channels, not replacements.
Removing a subprocessor requires 30-day advance notice to every workspace that has messages queued through it (SendGrid → SES migration path is documented in the compliance module's runbooks).

Every transport's inbound webhook must pass signature verification before writing InvitationEvent rows. Signature failures are counted (invitations.webhook.signature_failed.total metric) and alert above threshold.
